heading: Examination of outbound mail
label: 1583 Examination of outbound mail

Effective Date, , , provided that:Pub. L. 107–210, div. A, title III, § 344(c)116 Stat. 987Aug. 6, 2002“(1).—In generalExcept as provided in paragraph (2), this section [enacting this section and provisions set out as a note under this section] and the amendments made by this section shall take effect on the date of enactment of this Act [].Aug. 6, 2002“(2).—Certification with respect to foreign mailThe provisions of section 583 of the Tariff Act of 1930 [this section] relating to foreign mail transiting the United States that is imported or exported by the United States Postal Service shall not take effect until the Secretary of State certifies to Congress, pursuant to subsection (b) [set out as a note below], that the application of such section 583 is consistent with international law and any international obligation of the United States.”

Transfer of FunctionsFor transfer of functions, personnel, assets, and liabilities of the United States Customs Service of the Department of the Treasury, including functions of the Secretary of the Treasury relating thereto, to the Secretary of Homeland Security, and for treatment of related references, see sections 203(1), 551(d), 552(d), and 557 of Title 6, Domestic Security, and the Department of Homeland Security Reorganization Plan of , as modified, set out as a note under . For establishment of U.S. Customs and Border Protection in the Department of Homeland Security, treated as if included in as of , see , as amended generally by , and , set out as a note under .November 25, 2002Nov. 25, 2002section 542 of Title 6Pub. L. 107–296section 211 of Title 6Pub. L. 114–125section 802(b) of Pub. L. 114–125section 211 of Title 6

Certification by Secretary, , , provided that:Pub. L. 107–210, div. A, title III, § 344(b)116 Stat. 987Aug. 6, 2002“Not later than 3 months after the date of enactment of this section [], the Secretary of State shall determine whether the application of section 583 of the Tariff Act of 1930 [this section] to foreign mail transiting the United States that is imported or exported by the United States Postal Service is being handled in a manner consistent with international law and any international obligation of the United States. Section 583 of such Act shall not apply to such foreign mail unless the Secretary certifies to Congress that the application of such section 583 is consistent with international law and any international obligation of the United States.”Aug. 6, 2002

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: For purposes of ensuring compliance with the Customs laws of the United States and other laws enforced by the Customs Service, including the provisions of law described in paragraph (2), a Customs officer may, subject to the provisions of this section, stop and search at the border, without a search warrant, mail of domestic origin transmitted for export by the United States Postal Service and foreign mail transiting the United States that is being imported or exported by the United States Postal Service.

children.label: A
children.text: (relating to reports on exporting and importing monetary instruments).Section 5316 of title 31

children.label: B
children.text: Sections 1461, 1463, 1465, and 1466, and chapter 110 of title 18 (relating to obscenity and child pornography).

children.label: C
children.text: (relating to exportation of controlled substances).Section 953 of title 21

children.label: D
children.text: The Export Administration Act of 1979 (50 U.S.C. App. 2401 et seq.).1See References in Text note below.1

children.label: E
children.text: .Section 2778 of title 22

children.label: F
children.text: The International Emergency Economic Powers Act ( et seq.).50 U.S.C. 1701

children.heading: Search of mail not sealed against inspection and other mail
children.label: b Search of mail not sealed against inspection and other mail
children.text: Mail not sealed against inspection under the postal laws and regulations of the United States, mail which bears a Customs declaration, and mail with respect to which the sender or addressee has consented in writing to search, may be searched by a Customs officer.

children.heading: Search of mail sealed against inspection weighing in excess of 16 ounces
children.label: c Search of mail sealed against inspection weighing in excess of 16 ounces
A
children.text: Monetary instruments, as defined in .section 1956 of title 18

children.label: B
children.text: A weapon of mass destruction, as defined in section 2332a(b) of title 18.1

children.label: C
children.text: A drug or other substance listed in schedule I, II, III, or IV in .section 812 of title 21

children.label: D
children.text: National defense and related information transmitted in violation of any of sections 793 through 798 of title 18.

children.label: E
children.text: Merchandise mailed in violation of section 1715 or 1716 of title 18.

children.label: F
children.text: Merchandise mailed in violation of any provision of chapter 71 (relating to obscenity) or chapter 110 (relating to sexual exploitation and other abuse of children) of title 18.

children.label: G
children.text: Merchandise mailed in violation of the Export Administration Act of 1979 (50 U.S.C. App. 2401 et seq.).1

children.label: H
children.text: Merchandise mailed in violation of .section 2778 of title 22

children.label: I
children.text: Merchandise mailed in violation of the International Emergency Economic Powers Act ( et seq.).50 U.S.C. 1701

children.label: J
children.text: Merchandise mailed in violation of the Trading with the Enemy Act (50 U.S.C. App. 1 et seq.).1

children.label: K
children.text: Merchandise subject to any other law enforced by the Customs Service.

children.label: A
children.text: a search warrant has been issued pursuant to rule 41 of the Federal Rules of Criminal Procedure; or

children.label: B
children.text: the sender or addressee has given written authorization for such reading.

children.heading: Search of mail sealed against inspection weighing 16 ounces or less
children.label: d Search of mail sealed against inspection weighing 16 ounces or less
children.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of this section, subsection (a)(1) shall not apply to mail weighing 16 ounces or less sealed against inspection under the postal laws and regulations of the United States.
